title: #46 - The Amalfi Coast to lemon trees.

blocks.text: July 4th
Woke up and the beach I was camping on was becoming full of people. I suppose I slept in.
No one seemed to mind. Many people wanted to chat and thought it was interesting that I had a bike.
I biked back to Salerno for a coffee. And went for coffee with another Italian man that was biking with me for a bit. we chatted about life.
Then I biked north along the coast again.
I met another Italian cyclist who I followed for a little while. He was 83! It was actually hard to keep up with him. I wanted to stop to put sunscreen on and eat so he left me behind.

When I stopped I had a Methana like view and I sat with some old Italians while I made my Greek salad for lunch. I just melted away into chair with the view Methana vibes. It was really nice. I absolutely love staring out at the ocean.
Then I biked the Amalfi coast. Holy shit... I wish I had that GoPro. It was unreal to bike on.
I just went nice and slow stopping in towns that I was recommended to visit. I met some argentines. I got lunch with them and they bought me coffee!!! So nice of them.
I went for swims in the ocean, and enjoyed my time with the beautiful beaches and scenery. I could spend a month here on the beach.
Good place for a future, different type of vacation.
I'll let the pictures speak for themselves. I'm a broken record but yet again everything has been topped. This was the very best costal road I have biked on yet....
Then I got to Serrento. I had written in my notes a long time ago
"Go to Serrento"
But I forgot why I wrote that. Well I decided to go there to see it.
Here I am now, I made pasta and watched the sunset.
Now I am looking for a place to sleep.
I will write the rest of this after I find the place. Let's see what happens!

Okay here i am. I am writing the rest of this way after but I remember really well what happened the rest of the night. I started biking around Sorrento in the dark. It was really hard to find a place to camp in the city. At one point I saw this dark area that looked like no one was there. I left my bike there to go look for spots in the trees on foot. It was in Hilton parking lot and behind the parking lot that I found a flat hidden spot forest!

Jackpot. I thought I had found the spot. I had went away to brush my teeth and grab my bike. Taking my time thinking I was a genius for finding such a great camping spot. Then I went back with my bike and.... The gate to the parking lot is closed. I was locked out of the parking lot and my nice spot was now not available. At this point it was 11:30PM... oops. I kept trying to find a spot. I biked around aimlessly until I found by luck some lemon trees outside a parking garage. I camped there stealthy. I did not feel very hidden here so I planned on leaving early in the morning and I only set up my tent in a way so it would be less visible and block out the mosquitoes. The sleeping pad protected me from the sharp pokey thorns I had laid my tent on.
I notice that I keep getting bolder and bolder with my camping situations at night. Just you wait for the future posts. It's just hard to find spots in bigger cities. 🤣
-Bye for now, (Photos below)
Erik'

blocks.caption: Following the 85 year old showing me the start of the ride
Joining the Italians for a chat and the view
The view of the coast from the road.
More views of small cities on the road
The houses built into the cliffs look amazing
Someone offered to take a photo of me :)
Finally got to Sorrento for the sunset.
The nights accommodations hidden by lemon trees.
